highlights.text: I have for the last week, identified this range as a Bull Flag, writing yesterday: I wrote yesterday: “ 5734 to 5805 is a large consolidation, and this consolidation is a bull flag” On Friday, we rallied up to range resistance at 5805 and to start this week, my lean was that ranges do what they usually do - fill out, - and this case, to the downside.
I wrote yesterday that my lean for the day was: “As long as 5734 and 5746 hold, we can work back up to 5760, 5773, then 5786-88.
This played out to perfection today: At 630PM last night, we saw a quick Failed Breakdown below 5734, trapped shorts, then squeezed to 5805+ flag resistance by today’s close.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled For A Major Move Anytime Now. What Way? Oct 9 plan
